MOBILE LOGIN INCIDENT RAW MULTI-AGENT TRANSCRIPT

This file intentionally models the noisy work log a generalist LLM would have
to read if no durable kernel memory existed. The use case is an incident that
requires many failed solution attempts before the effective rollback is chosen.
Every worklog chunk below is one attempted diagnosis or mitigation, with the
agent hypothesis, action, tool output, observed result, and discard rationale.
The compact kernel contexts next to this file are scoped memory reconstructions
of the durable board, not a summary of this raw transcript after the fact.

--- FAILED ATTEMPT 1: attempt-0001 ---

[mobile-agent][10:1] hypothesis
Hypothesis under test: mobile retry storm exhausted the session refresh path.
The agent writes the full reasoning trail into the shared transcript because
there is no durable typed memory boundary in this raw baseline. The same
incident facts are repeated for later agents: iOS users enter valid credentials,
receive a token-shaped response, and land back on the login screen.

[mobile-agent][action]
Attempt attempt-0001: enabled stricter mobile retry backoff.
Expected result: iOS login failure rate should fall toward the 2% baseline if
this hypothesis is the active cause.

[mobile-agent][tool-output]
{"attempt":"attempt-0001","metric":"ios_login_failure_rate","value":"32%","baseline":"2%","window":"5m","shard":"ios-1","request":"mob-100001"}

[auth-agent][tool-output]
{"attempt":"attempt-0001","service":"auth-api","status":"401_spike","count_over_baseline":"24x","client":"ios","sample_token_shape":"legacy_audience","request":"auth-100001"}

[mobile-agent][observed-result]
Observed result: repeated attempts dropped but valid iOS logins still failed. The attempt is recorded as failed, not discarded,
because later audit needs to know why the team did not stop here.

[auth-agent][handoff]
Handoff after attempt-0001: keep jwt-parser-v3 rollout in the suspect set
because it happened before the first user-facing reports. Keep failed attempts
in the record: retry controls, scale-out, cache/JWKS actions, routing changes,
WAF changes, region shifts, and pool tuning have not explained recovery.

[infra-agent][distractor]
Nearby cache deploy remains visible in the raw transcript. For attempt-0001,
infra metrics show no broad Android impact and no matching backend latency
increase. The distractor remains because raw transcripts do not enforce scope.

[coordinator-agent][handoff-note]
Current working summary after attempt-0001: mobile failures plus Auth API 401s
plus parser rollout still point toward a compatibility regression in
jwt-parser-v3, but the team has not yet accepted rollback until enough failed
attempts make alternatives less likely.

--- FAILED ATTEMPT 2: attempt-0002 ---

[auth-agent][10:2] hypothesis
Hypothesis under test: Auth API capacity saturation caused token validation timeouts.
The agent writes the full reasoning trail into the shared transcript because
there is no durable typed memory boundary in this raw baseline. The same
incident facts are repeated for later agents: iOS users enter valid credentials,
receive a token-shaped response, and land back on the login screen.

[auth-agent][action]
Attempt attempt-0002: scaled Auth API replicas.
Expected result: iOS login failure rate should fall toward the 2% baseline if
this hypothesis is the active cause.

[mobile-agent][tool-output]
{"attempt":"attempt-0002","metric":"ios_login_failure_rate","value":"33%","baseline":"2%","window":"5m","shard":"ios-2","request":"mob-100002"}

[auth-agent][tool-output]
{"attempt":"attempt-0002","service":"auth-api","status":"401_spike","count_over_baseline":"25x","client":"ios","sample_token_shape":"legacy_audience","request":"auth-100002"}

[auth-agent][observed-result]
Observed result: latency stayed healthy but the 401 rate was unchanged. The attempt is recorded as failed, not discarded,
because later audit needs to know why the team did not stop here.

[release-agent][handoff]
Handoff after attempt-0002: keep jwt-parser-v3 rollout in the suspect set
because it happened before the first user-facing reports. Keep failed attempts
in the record: retry controls, scale-out, cache/JWKS actions, routing changes,
WAF changes, region shifts, and pool tuning have not explained recovery.

[infra-agent][distractor]
Nearby cache deploy remains visible in the raw transcript. For attempt-0002,
infra metrics show no broad Android impact and no matching backend latency
increase. The distractor remains because raw transcripts do not enforce scope.

[coordinator-agent][handoff-note]
Current working summary after attempt-0002: mobile failures plus Auth API 401s
plus parser rollout still point toward a compatibility regression in
jwt-parser-v3, but the team has not yet accepted rollback until enough failed
attempts make alternatives less likely.

--- FAILED ATTEMPT 3: attempt-0003 ---

[release-agent][10:3] hypothesis
Hypothesis under test: edge cache served stale JWKS material to iOS clients.
The agent writes the full reasoning trail into the shared transcript because
there is no durable typed memory boundary in this raw baseline. The same

receive a token-shaped response, and land back on the login screen.

[release-agent][action]

Expected result: iOS login failure rate should fall toward the 2% baseline if
this hypothesis is the active cause.

[mobile-agent][tool-output]
{"attempt":"attempt-0003","metric":"ios_login_failure_rate","value":"34%","baseline":"2%","window":"5m","shard":"ios-3","request":"mob-100003"}

[auth-agent][tool-output]
{"attempt":"attempt-0003","service":"auth-api","status":"401_spike","count_over_baseline":"26x","client":"ios","sample_token_shape":"legacy_audience","request":"auth-100003"}

[release-agent][observed-result]
Observed result: cache hit ratio reset but the iOS-only 401 pattern remained. The attempt is recorded as failed, not discarded,
because later audit needs to know why the team did not stop here.

Handoff after attempt-0003: keep jwt-parser-v3 rollout in the suspect set
